creator: kris@pcbsd.com

> 
> It works OK. Does that problem appears only when starting game? It 
> seems, it hangs only because /home/kris/.btanks/bt.xml doesn't exist.
> ([10:19:35.061][src/config.cpp:39] [error] load: [mrt/file.cpp:36]: 
> fopen("/home/kris/.btanks/bt.xml", "rt"): No such file or directory)
> 
> 
> I attach my bt.xml file, try to use this one.
> 

Ok, I did some further testing on this. Using your bt.xml file, did get 
the game to startup now. However, the first few maps I tried to play 
caused the game to crash again, with these errors:

[10:01:18.742][src/main.cpp:73]  [error] main: 
[src/game_monitor.cpp:677]: this map requires lua scripting support.
[10:01:18.742][src/main.cpp:73]  [debug] calling Game->deinit()
[10:01:18.742][src/game.cpp:729]         [debug] cleaning up main game 
object...




I looked into the Makefile, and saw that the option for LUA was set to 
off. I enabled it and rebuilt the game. Now I can play games just fine.

Since all the maps I tried require LUA, it would probably be best to set 
it to "on" by default. Also, how can we fix or get around the missing 
bt.xml files? Thats kind a showstopper if the user cant run the game 
because its missing. Does your game run if you remove ~/.btanks/bt.xml?




-- 

Kris Moore
PC-BSD Software
http://www.pcbsd.com
